description: Switchable Crypto Price Browser Tab - Allow users to switch between any Coinbase-supported cryptocurrency, one coin at a time, with persistent selection and clean UI

userStories.title: Replace page icon with coin code label
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the current coin's ticker code displayed prominently on the page so I know which coin I'm monitoring.
acceptanceCriteria: The BTC element is replaced with a large, styled text element showing the current coin code (e.g. 'BTC')
The text label is visually prominent and centered, matching the previous icon layout
The static BTC favicon in the <link> tag remains unchanged

userStories.title: Show coin code in tab title alongside price
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the coin code alongside the price in the browser tab title so I always know which coin I'm tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab title format is 'CODE $price' (e.g. 'BTC $68432')
The price continues to update every 5 seconds

userStories.title: Make coin code and price update dynamically based on a variable
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the fetch URL, page label, and tab title to use a configurable coin code variable so switching coins is possible.
acceptanceCriteria: A currentCoin variable controls which coin is fetched from Coinbase API
The fetch URL uses the currentCoin variable: /v2/prices/{currentCoin}-USD/spot
The coin code label on the page reflects currentCoin
The tab title reflects currentCoin
Changing currentCoin and calling the update function fetches the new coin's price

userStories.title: Persist selected coin in localStorage
userStories.description: As a user, I want my selected coin to be remembered across page reloads so I don't have to re-select it every time.
acceptanceCriteria: On page load, the app reads 'selectedCoin' from localStorage
If no value is saved, the app defaults to 'BTC'
When the coin is changed, the new coin code is saved to localStorage
If the saved coin fails to fetch on load, the app falls back to BTC

userStories.title: Click coin label to reveal search input
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click the coin label to reveal a text input so I can type a new coin ticker code.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the coin code label reveals a text input field below or in place of the label
The input is hidden by default - only the coin label and price are visible
Pressing Escape or clicking outside the input hides it without changing the coin
The input field is focused automatically when revealed
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Validate and switch coin on input submit
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to validate my coin code against Coinbase and switch to it if valid, or show an error if not.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Enter in the input triggers a fetch to /v2/prices/{CODE}-USD/spot with the uppercased input value
If the API returns a successful response with price data, the app switches to that coin and hides the input
If the API returns an error, 'Coin not found' is displayed inline below the input
The current coin continues to be monitored while the error is displayed
The error message disappears when the user starts typing again or closes the input
On successful switch, the new coin code is saved to localStorage
The setInterval for price polling is cleared and restarted for the new coin
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
